metrics.narrative: Zycus presented quantified ROI claims (40% sourcing cycle-time reduction, 60% enhanced bids, 3x faster stakeholder engagement, 2x faster procurement cycle) on 18 Dec 2025, and Karen Pagorek pushed back directly: "that seems pretty optimistic... I need to reset based on what our current environment is." Arthur Raguette committed to a benchmark/value-engineering sheet to validate the numbers, but no confirmation of delivery or buyer sign-off on revised figures has surfaced since. Separately, the rep's Next Step (~31 Mar 2026) notes "POC for ANA and Spend was positive," a real but unquantified signal that the tools performed well in practice.

champion.narrative: Karen Pagorek is the closest thing to a champion: she has driven the cadence of calls (28 Jan, 24 Feb, 31 Mar, 11 May 2026) and pushed rigorously on ROI validation, which shows genuine engagement. However, there is no evidence she is advocating internally to Eduardo Viera or Joshua Jepsen; the rep's own notes describe needing to "force the Eduardo connection" rather than being bridged by the relevant stakeholder is also the one who paused the POC on 11 May 2026, which is a mixed signal for a champion.

competition.narrative: Multiple threats are on record: GEP is explicitly named as the target of the Spend Analysis pilot ("Pilot Spend Analysis to counter GEP"), and the do-nothing/internal-build path (TCS homegrown solution plus OpenAI/Ultrametrics partnerships) has been the backdrop since the original 24 Jun 2025 discovery call. The most current and most serious threat is an unidentified internal I/O provider that Karen Pagorek said John Deere is doing a "deep dive" on as of 11 May 2026, which directly triggered the POC pause. Oracle and SAP Ariba appear only in the Salesforce Competitors field with no corroborating call evidence found.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated. Karen Powers described John Deere's homegrown TCS quoting tool and described customizing purchased solutions ("deorizing") as "a nightmare," citing difficulty tracking cost changes over time and a lack of should-cost modeling and BOM/PLM integration (24 Jun 2025 demo). Eduardo Viera separately confirmed 2024 funding was secured against hard-savings ROI to accelerate automation (30 Sep 2025).

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, Order Form, SOW, or contracting discussion has occurred in any read source. The deal remains in a pre-commercial pilot/proof-of-value phase at Shortlisted; the only pricing conversation to date was a budgetary, non-final proposal reviewed on 18 Dec 2025.

economic_buyer.narrative: Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Supply Management Indirect Materials and Services) is the Salesforce-listed Executive Sponsor and had a substantive 1:1 with Arthur Raguette on 30 Sep 2025, discussing partnership structure, confirmed 2024 funding, and a phased approach, and stating he was not pursuing a competitive tender. He has not appeared on any call since (~9 months). Joshua Jepsen (SVP & CFO), also a listed Executive Sponsor, has never engaged in any recorded interaction. Next Step notes explicitly flag the gap: "Track Eduardo Viera - Indirect Procurement - need a Jolt to action" and "force the Eduardo connection."

decision_process.narrative: The process runs through Karen Pagorek (day-to-day evaluator) and Eduardo Viera (executive sponsor), with Next Step notes stating "verified path to agreement is through Karen Pagorek and Eduardo Viera." Delivery moved through discovery, a full demo, a partnership 1:1, two on-site workshops (Nov 2025), and a detailed pricing/scoping call (Dec 2025), followed by a proof-of-value phase (Mar 2026). No decision date or formal down-select timeline has been confirmed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are forming around a bounded, benchmark-driven evaluation: a three-pronged pilot structure (Spend Analysis to counter GEP, iSource for strategic sourcing with limited scope, ANA for tactical MRO) with a target scorecard already drafted by the rep. Karen Pagorek also asked for precise definitional boundaries between iSource/e-auctions and agent-driven autonomous sourcing on 18 Dec 2025, indicating criteria are still being pinned down rather than finalized.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A well-worked, technically-validated deal (positive ANA and Spend Analysis proof-of-value results) that has stalled at the executive and commercial level: the champion paused the POC pending a review of an internal alternative, the economic buyer has been silent for ~9 months, and the CFO has never engaged.

deal_risk.text: A live competitive/build-vs-buy review (the unidentified internal I/O provider) is actively deciding the deal's fate as of 11 May 2026.
Executive access is thin: Eduardo Viera has gone quiet for ~9 months and Joshua Jepsen has never engaged, leaving no confirmed path to budget approval.

win_position.text: The proof-of-value for ANA and Spend Analysis was reported positive by the rep around 31 Mar 2026, showing real technical validation beyond a generic demo.

Karen Powers described Zycus's AI-enabled sourcing and autonomous negotiation as 'really cool' on 24 Jun 2025, and pain around the homegrown TCS tool and 'deorizing' custom solutions is clearly and repeatedly confirmed.

Karen Pagorek paused the POC on 11 May 2026 pending a deep dive on an unidentified internal I/O provider, with the rep's own notes naming the ramifications as 'on hold, in-house build, or competitive loss.'
No economic buyer engagement has occurred since Eduardo Viera's 30 Sep 2025 1:1, and CFO Joshua Jepsen (listed Executive Sponsor) has never appeared in any call across the deal's history.

deal_momentum.text: A buyer-side email reply was received on 12 Jun 2026, the first two-way contact since the POC pause, and Karen Pagorek has accepted a reconnect for 20 Jul 2026.
The proof-of-concept was explicitly paused on 11 May 2026 and no forward commercial or paper-process activity has occurred since; the close date remains parked far out at 22 Feb 2027.

hard.ais_why:
• On the 12/18/2025 call, champion Karen Pagorek pushed Arthur to define autonomous and agentic terms more precisely, specifically asking him to disentangle e-auctions, tactical spend, and autonomous sourcing with negotiation inside iSource. That terminology pressure is substantive AI dialogue from the buyer side, not surface-level interest.
• Karen then asked what a pilot might look like. Arthur proposed pilots covering iSource, autonomous sourcing, and spend analysis: a sandbox RFX event, an MRO category negotiation, and a 3-month spend cube prototype. Karen also pressure-tested ROI realism on the $48M savings claim, calling for transparency on the assumptions.
• The offsetting signal is that the three-prong AI POC was paused on 5/11/26. That is a real down-signal post-call. The buyer is still at "quantify incremental value" and remembers poor feedback from past supplier evaluations. Active autonomous-sourcing dialogue offsets the pause, but the pause is why this stays in the cautious band.
